photo_analysis.photo_facade_colour: warm red-brown brick upper storey, dark-painted ground floor storefront with black metal framing
photo_analysis.photo_notes: Night photo. Narrow 2-storey commercial on Kensington Ave pedestrian strip. Typical converted Victorian house-form with ground-floor retail. Adjacent to similar row of converted houses. Party wall visible on right side (shared with neighbour).

photo_analysis.photo_facade_colour: mixed: blue-painted glass storefront at ground level, brown/red brick upper floor, white-painted wood gable peak
photo_analysis.photo_notes: Converted house-form per HCD typology. Commercial ground floor with glass storefront. Upper floor retains Victorian character with original brick and fenestration. Front gable is legible.
No dedicated facade photo in this batch. Part of 16-18 Kensington Ave semi-detached pair. Shared roofline with central gable.
No dedicated facade photo in this batch. Part of 20-22 Kensington Ave pair. Large painted mural covers most of facade per existing assessment.

photo_analysis.photo_facade_colour: dark brown/black painted ground floor with blue-lit storefront windows; exposed red-brown brick on upper floors; large painted mural above storefront

Night photo (IMG_20260320_012906257_HDR.jpg), rotated. 2-storey commercial. Victorian gable peak visible above commercial addition. Adjacent to 42 Kensington Ave (Butterfly).
Only partially visible in IMG_20260320_012906257_HDR.jpg (adjacent to 40 Kensington Ave). Limited visibility; most details inferred from adjacency to 40. Low confidence due to partial view.

photo_analysis.photo_facade_colour: tan/beige painted wood clapboard siding on upper floor, dark brown/black commercial ground floor
photo_analysis.photo_notes: Converted House-form per HCD typology. Style corrected from Vernacular to Victorian - the underlying house-form with gable roof dates to the Victorian era despite wood clapboard cladding alteration. Commercial ground floor is expected for this typology.

photo_analysis.photo_facade_colour: black painted ground floor with large graffiti mural; white/cream cladding visible on adjacent structure; number 75 in yellow
photo_analysis.photo_notes: matches_hcd corrected from false to true. Style corrected from Vernacular to Victorian. Despite heavy street-level alterations (black paint, graffiti), the house-form with gable roof is still legible. The semi-detached pair form is consistent with the 1904-1913 construction date.
